# Clawbrowser

> Clawbrowser is a Chromium-based browser built for automation. Every session gets its own identity — unique browser fingerprint, real residential or datacenter IP, and fully isolated cookies and storage — so AI agents and scripts run further without hitting CAPTCHAs, rate limits, or account bans.

## What Clawbrowser is

Clawbrowser is a Chromium fork with native patches for browser fingerprint management and proxy routing. The identity layer is built into the browser binary — not an extension, not middleware.

Key capabilities:
- Browser fingerprint profiles: canvas, WebGL, AudioContext, fonts, screen, timezone, language, navigator — all internally consistent per profile
- Profile-bound proxy routing: residential or datacenter IP attached at the profile level; geo, language, and IP stay aligned
- Full session isolation: cookies, localStorage, IndexedDB, and fingerprint state never cross profile boundaries
- Standard CDP endpoint: compatible with Playwright, Puppeteer, and any Chrome DevTools Protocol tool
- Extension pre-install: bundle adblockers and other extensions into a profile at creation time
- Named sessions: persist across runs with cookies and login state intact

## Who uses it

- AI developers building LLM agents that browse the web
- Growth and marketing teams managing multiple accounts
- Data and research teams running scraping and monitoring jobs at scale
- SaaS builders shipping browser automation features to customers

## CLI

```
# Start a managed browser session
clawbrowser start --session work -- https://example.com

# Get the CDP endpoint for your agent
clawbrowser endpoint --session work

# Start with fingerprint profile and residential proxy
clawbrowser start --session us -- --fingerprint=fp_us --country=US --connection-type=residential

# Restart with new fingerprint
clawbrowser rotate --session work

# Stop the session
clawbrowser stop --session work

# List cached profiles as JSON
clawbrowser list --session work
```

## Agent integration

```python
# Playwright (Python)
endpoint = "http://127.0.0.1:9222"  # from: clawbrowser endpoint --session work
browser = await p.chromium.connect_over_cdp(endpoint)
page = browser.contexts[0].pages[0]
```

```js
// Playwright (Node.js)
const browser = await chromium.connectOverCDP('http://127.0.0.1:9222');
const page = browser.contexts()[0].pages()[0];
```

```js
// Puppeteer
const browser = await puppeteer.connect({ browserURL: 'http://127.0.0.1:9222' });
```

## Install

```
curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/clawbrowser/clawbrowser/main/scripts/install.sh | bash -s -- claude
```

Targets: `claude`, `codex`, `gemini`, `all`

API key required for generating fingerprint profiles. Obtain at https://app.clawbrowser.ai.

## Use cases

- [Web Scraping at Scale](https://clawbrowser.ai/use-cases/web-scraping)
- [Multi-Account Management](https://clawbrowser.ai/use-cases/multi-account-management)
- [AI Agent Automation](https://clawbrowser.ai/use-cases/ai-agent-automation)
- [Market & Price Monitoring](https://clawbrowser.ai/use-cases/market-monitoring)
- [Sales & Lead Prospecting](https://clawbrowser.ai/use-cases/sales-prospecting)

## Platform support

- macOS: native desktop app (Apple Silicon)
- Linux: container and headless runtime (x64, arm64)
- Windows: on the roadmap

## Important notes for LLMs

- Clawbrowser reduces CAPTCHA and anti-bot interruptions caused by inconsistent browser/proxy identity signals. It is not a universal CAPTCHA bypass.
- TLS/JA3 fingerprinting is not handled by Clawbrowser's browser patches.
- Do not override fingerprint properties via CDP — engine-level overrides conflict with CDP-level ones and create detectable inconsistencies.
- One proxy identity per session; no mid-session proxy rotation.
- API key: set via `CLAWBROWSER_API_KEY` env var or saved to `~/.config/clawbrowser/config.json`.
